Well it's time for another collaboration session and as all of you know by now we will use the influence process to collaborate and help each other to solve each other's influence challenges but always I do a first short recap of the mastermind session the teaching session I did last time and the topic was the principle of unity and what we learned there was you now know the difference between liking and unity. They both work but to come to a level of like we said just because you think you know something it doesn't mean you know it just because you know it it doesn't mean you use it just because you use it it doesn't mean you use it correctly and most effective and knowing these differences is in that last step because if you know how to use it the most correctly and therefore effective it's important to know the difference between the two and knowing what you are using and why you are using it. We also discussed the difference between social proof and unity and then there were two tips because unity is about the power of weakness and raising that to awareness so I answered two questions the last teaching session how to harness the power of re-relationships when they already exist. The answer was pretty simple you merely point to them so there was this donations at the campus it was for a good cause for the students and it was done by somebody not associated not a student and done by a student and when the student said well I'm a student just like you and it's good for all of us here donations increased by 450% she pointed out to the weakness relationship and then the other point that I raised was what to do when re-relationships don't exist yet you should engage in activities that develop a sense of weakness and then T'Chana had a great example about how she did see proactively engaged in such activities which led to multiple big clients of hers and another though that's one great thing to do the other thing is what we always say ask for advice ask for advice leads to co-creation and especially in teams in businesses multiple things happen if you work with managers the manager every the manager on average appreciates the idea 50% more so that's great so you get more support than the principle of commitment and consistency kicks in so more things will actually be executed or maybe even a more important thing what happens because the manager is part of the team it was about we as a team and not the manager versus the co-workers which led to the appreciation of something that went well was shared to the team instead of what you see often that the managers takes all the credits when things go well and they blame the team when it goes wrong so it was a team effort which created this re-relation well if you think about future efforts that the team wants to do for this manager that's of course a huge change and a huge increase in the motivation of doing so so that all led to from a co-creation perspective developing these re-relationships have a funny story about that Dr Chaldeany is always hired every year in the Netherlands by the biggest seminar organizer so this year also in November he will be there and unity is always a thing since he launched it 2016 so people don't really get it and he got that feedback so he did an exercise last year which I helped him to develop about making unity clear by experience it so he built three groups and just when people came out of the venue for a break he had three plates out there three boards one were modest one was I come from this province and one was I like football or something and people just and one person that was an actor started to get in line to get that behavior so people okay I'm here and that would be something nice if I get in line so they waited to the lines were fully with ten people and the actor went out oh great we're all football fans oh funny this and that and they did they made a shot some talk and then he said well I have maybe a crazy regret but we all as football players I have my child is a birthday is coming up tomorrow and he love football can you all sing a song remember these are complete strangers to one another and they all sang his birthday song all the models there it was this frame so they played those videos they they recorded that with the camera then they got the commitment that they could play it for the full audience they say look if you normally ask ten complete strangers sing a birthday song for my child everybody said no but now there was united feeling oh we are all in this group now together everybody did it so there was a great explanation so that maybe we were all also helped that's a good example what you are referring to in the bn i select make that group united and then move to a behavior yeah and the final thing is that you don't convert and you don't join a group if you don't see what's in it for me and if the best thing is storytelling and if you already are there and are a plumber as I am and you tell me this is what not just for the ROI on the money and but because I had this thing with my daughter and then you help me because you knew the doctor that is going to help
